purpose: Vivi Storybook is the fast UI regression lab and facade-review surface for the local review adapter. Every product-facing UI slice should be represented by at least one stable story and, when the behavior is interactive, one representative interaction story.

reviewStages.label: HTML Concept Mock
reviewStages.intent: Use docs/ui-mocks to compare layout, density, information hierarchy, and product direction before React implementation.

reviewStages.label: Storybook Facade
reviewStages.intent: Use React components with static props, simplified callbacks, and domain-shaped fixtures as the design approval surface before real application, backend, filesystem, SSE, route, or preview behavior is wired.

reviewStages.label: Wired Feature
reviewStages.intent: After explicit approval, connect the approved facade to real application state, use cases, infrastructure, and server behavior while preserving useful facade stories as visual contracts.

surfaces.label: Design review flow
surfaces.intent: Make Vivi's HTML Concept Mock -> Storybook Facade -> Wired Feature workflow visible inside Storybook itself.
reviewFocus: reviewers can see why a facade story may be static
agents can identify the current GUI review stage
Storybook communicates the distinction between visual approval and wired behavior

surfaces.label: Workspace shell
surfaces.intent: Preserve the classic local workspace frame: topbar, live tree, tabs, active viewer, right inspector, and status states.
reviewFocus: sidebar remains the stable spatial map
tabs make multi-file context visible
active viewer is visually primary
right inspector is available without competing with reading

surfaces.label: Review navigation
surfaces.intent: Keep Review Queue, comments inbox, unread activity, and agent replies visible as the primary right-inspector workflow.
reviewFocus: open review work is easier to find than completed history
agent activity is visible without creating false unread work
queue rows expose enough metadata to choose the next file

surfaces.label: Comment lifecycle
surfaces.intent: Represent open, resolved, draft, published, failure, agent-read/reply, and archive-action states without requiring a backend. Archived threads are hidden from the browser UI after the transition.
reviewFocus: draft comments read as private until publish
open and resolved threads are visually distinct
failure and published states are recoverable and obvious

surfaces.label: Viewer coverage
surfaces.intent: Cover every product viewer surface: Markdown, HTML, code, JSON, CSV/text, Mermaid, image, unknown text, large text, and binary metadata.
reviewFocus: viewer kind is obvious without implying editing
fallback states remain readable and safe
toolbar controls stay stable across file types

surfaces.label: Diff review
surfaces.intent: Keep source diffs, rendered Markdown/HTML diffs, unavailable diffs, binary diffs, and diff comments visible.
reviewFocus: added, removed, and unavailable diff states are easy to distinguish
diff comments stay anchored to the reviewed surface
rendered diff review remains readable beside source diff review

surfaces.label: Navigation overlays
surfaces.intent: Cover Cmd/Ctrl+K quick open, text search, command mode, shortcut help, topbar, tree, and tabs.
reviewFocus: overlays feel modal rather than permanent layout
keyboard focus and dismissal behavior are reviewable
search results make the next action obvious

surfaces.label: Stress and empty states
surfaces.intent: Make dense review sessions, empty workspaces, long lists, loading states, and missing results easy to inspect before E2E.
reviewFocus: empty states explain what is absent without marketing copy
dense states remain scannable
long lists preserve stable layout and scrolling

agentWorkflow: Use HTML Concept Mock -> Storybook Facade -> Wired Feature for meaningful GUI changes unless the user explicitly asks to skip it.
Read ui/src/storybook/README.md before changing product UI.
Confirm whether the current work is in the html-concept, storybook-facade, or wired-feature stage.
Update or add a story for every user-visible UI state you changed.
Facade stories may use static props, simplified callbacks, and domain-shaped fixtures; they must not mock filesystem watchers, HTTP routes, GraphQL transport, SSE, or real preview server behavior.
If the UI introduces a new product-facing surface, add it to this manifest.
Add a play interaction for one representative story when the change affects keyboard, pointer, focus, or form behavior.
Run task storybook:verify and task storybook:build before handing off UI changes.
